messages.content:
How is the layer of oil removed from the surface in an oily water separator on ships?


a.By using chemicals


b. By sedimentation


c. By skimming


d. By filtration
c. By skimming

Which mechanism is used to remove the floating layer of oil from the surface in an oily water separator on ships?


a. Chemical injection


b. Filtration


c. Sedimentation


d. Skimming
d. Skimming

What is the purpose of post-treatment processes in an oily water separator on ships?


a. To produce drinking water


b. To store the water for later use


c. To generate steam


d. To remove oil from the water
d. To remove oil from the water

What is the main purpose of an oily water separator?


a. To disinfect water


b. To separate oil and water mixtures


c. To generate electricity


d. To remove solid waste from water
b. To separate oil and water mixtures

Which industry commonly uses oily water separators?


Which industry commonly uses oily water separators?


a. Agriculture


b. Oil refineries


c. Food processing


d. Construction
b. Oil refineries

How is the layer of oil removed from the surface of the separator?


a. By adding chemicals


b. By using a skimming mechanism


c. By applying heat


d. By filtration
b. By using a skimming mechanism

What should the crew do if they suspect a sensor malfunction in the oily water separator?


a. Adjust the separator settings.


b. Ignore the issue and continue operations.


c. Inspect and repair or replace the faulty sensor.


d. Replace the entire separator system.
c. Inspect and repair or replace the faulty sensor.

How is the layer of oil removed from the surface in an oily water separator?


a. By using chemicals


b. By sedimentation


c. By skimming


d. By filtration
c. By skimming

What is the purpose of coalescing plates or media in an oily water separator?


a. To generate electricity


b. To remove solid waste


c. To heat the mixture


d. To aggregate oil droplets
d. To aggregate oil droplets

What happens to the treated water after it exits the oily water separator?


a. It is stored in a fuel tank


b. It is used for engine cooling


c. It is discharged overboard


d. It is converted into steam
c. It is discharged overboard

How can improper adjustment of separator settings contribute to oil carryover?


a. It can cause an oil-water emulsion.


b. It can lead to clogged filters.


c. It can result in excessive water discharge.


d. It can increase oil storage capacity.
a. It can cause an oil-water emulsion.

What is the function of a skimming mechanism in an oily water separator?


a. To disinfect the water


b. To regulate the water flow


c. To collect the separated oil


d. To remove solid particles
c. To collect the separated oil

Which force causes the larger oil droplets to rise to the surface during separation?


Which force causes the larger oil droplets to rise to the surface during separation?


Gravity


Electrostatic force


Centrifugal force


Magnetic force
Gravity
